About This Project

Forest Fire Prediction Using Artificial Intelligence
Graduation Project - Fire and Safety Engineering

Project Overview

This system is an AI-powered early warning tool designed to predict and map forest fire risks across Jordan. By analyzing over 120,000 fire incident records from 2018 to 2025, our machine learning models can identify high-risk areas and time periods, enabling civil protection authorities to take proactive measures.

How It Works

1. Data Collection

Historical fire incident data from across Jordan, including coordinates, fire types, dates, and locations.

2. Processing

Coordinate conversion, feature engineering, and data cleaning to prepare for ML model training.

3. ML Models

Multiple algorithms (Random Forest, XGBoost, SVM, Neural Networks) trained to predict fire risk levels.

4. Early Warning

Interactive dashboard and risk maps enable authorities to allocate resources and prevent disasters.

Key Features

  • Interactive Fire Map - View all incidents with clustering and heatmap visualization
  • Risk Prediction - Predict fire risk for any region and month using AI models
  • Statistical Dashboards - Analyze trends by year, month, type, and location
  • Model Comparison - Evaluate performance of different ML algorithms
  • Mobile Responsive - Accessible on desktop, tablet, and mobile devices

Technology Stack

Frontend

ML / Data Science
scikit-learn XGBoost pandas numpy

Prince Al Hussein Bin Abdallah II Academy

Fire and Safety Engineering Department
Graduation Project 2025

Project Goal

Develop an AI-powered early warning system to predict and prevent forest fires across Jordan, protecting natural resources and communities.

Impact

By enabling data-driven decision making, this system helps civil protection authorities allocate resources effectively and reduce fire-related damages.
